input.description:
Use this tool when:
The user refers to an existing draft but does not provide a draft ID.
The user asks to view, list, or select from their drafts.
Do not use this tool:
To automatically choose a draft to send.
As a background step when a draft is already present in the conversational context.
input.parameters_schema_json: [{"name":"query","type":"string","optional":true,"hint":"Only return draft messages matching the specified query. Supports the same query format as the Gmail search box. For example, \"from:someuser@example.com rfc822msgid:<somemsgid@example.com> is:unread\".","control_type":"text"},{"name":"pageToken","type":"string","optional":true,"hint":"Page token to retrieve a specific page of results in the list.","control_type":"text"},{"name":"maxResults","type":"integer","optional":true,"hint":"Maximum number of drafts to return. This field defaults to 30. The maximum allowed value for this field is 50.","control_type":"integer"}]

properties.label: Query
properties.name: query
properties.hint: Only return draft messages matching the specified query. Supports the same query format as the Gmail search box. For example, "from:someuser@example.com rfc822msgid:<somemsgid@example.com> is:unread".

properties.label: Page token
properties.name: pageToken
properties.hint: Page token to retrieve a specific page of results in the list.

properties.label: Max results
properties.name: maxResults
properties.hint: Maximum number of drafts to return. This field defaults to 30. The maximum allowed value for this field is 50.
